problem_statement: Searching flights with an origin airport code in lowercase (e.g. `origin=ath`) returns no matches even though flights exist for that airport. The search logic appears to compare the provided origin value case-sensitively against airport codes stored in uppercase.
goal: Make flight origin search case-insensitive so that `GET /flights/search?origin=ath` matches flights whose origin airport code is stored as `ATH` (and similarly for other casings).
acceptance_criteria: `GET /flights/search?origin=ath` returns the same results as `origin=ATH` when matching flights exist.
Origin filtering continues to work for uppercase input.
Existing search behavior for other filters is unchanged unless they share the same normalization issue.
constraints: Keep the fix localized to the flight search implementation if possible.
Airport codes are stored in uppercase, so normalize the incoming search term before matching or compare in a case-insensitive way.
unknowns: Whether destination filtering or other airport-code filters share the same case-sensitivity bug.
Whether the fix should be implemented in the service layer only or also mirrored in tests/API route validation.
repo_summary: Backend service appears to own flight search logic; likely route `backend/app/api/routes/flights.py` delegates to `backend/app/services/flight_service.py`. Tests exist in `backend/app/tests/test_flight_service.py`.

targeted_symbols.reason: This is the affected implementation named in the issue; likely contains the origin filter comparison that is currently case-sensitive.
Add or update a regression test covering lowercase origin input matching uppercase airport codes.

localization_rationale: The issue explicitly names `backend/app/services/flight_service.py (search_flights)` as the affected file.
The symptom is narrowly scoped to origin airport search casing, which strongly suggests a single comparison/normalization bug inside the service method.
The most relevant follow-up area is the service test module to capture the regression.
